title: Contributing to AquaSensR
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("massbays-tech/AquaSensR", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: AquaSensR inputs and checks
text: Use readASRcont() import continuous monitoring data. function reads Excel file, automatically runs series checks via checkASRcont(), formats result downstream use. tz argument sets time zone output DateTime column (see OlsonNames() valid values). careful specify correct time zone, particularly one using fixed offset (e.g., Etc/GMT+5 Eastern time) avoid issues daylight saving time transitions. data local time time zone observes DST, consider using time zone like America/New_York automatically adjust daylight savings. AquaSensR accepts two input formats date time information. examples demonstrate . Format 1 — separate Date Time columns (ExampleCont1.xlsx): Format 2 — combined DateTime column (ExampleCont2.xlsx): calls return identically structured output (see Output format ). continuous monitoring data workbook must follow one two accepted schemas. Additional unrecognised columns trigger error. Format 1: separate Date Time columns Format 2: combined DateTime column Currently, AquaSensR allows following parameters. Note inclusion units parameter name. Make sure parameter name matches units used data. list can also viewed R paramsASR dataset, included package used checks. readASRcont() function imports data runs series checks using checkASRcont() function. checks evaluate following stops informative error check fails: Column names: columns either Date, Time, DateTime, recognised parameter paramsASR. Required columns present: either Date Time (Format 1) DateTime (Format 2). least one parameter column: least one column matches entry paramsASR$Parameter. Date format (Format 1 ): values Date parse successfully lubridate::ymd(). Time format (Format 1 ): values Time parseable lubridate::parse_date_time() 24-hour, 12-hour /PM, Excel-native formats. DateTime format (Format 2 ): values DateTime parseable lubridate::parse_date_time() 24-hour 12-hour /PM formats. missing values: NA column. Numeric parameter columns: parameter columns contain numeric values. Adding unrecognised column causes checkASRcont() stop immediately. following examples demonstrate formats. passing checks, readASRcont() returns data frame structure regardless input format: DateTime: time-zone-aware POSIXct column One numeric column per parameter present input file

title: Quality control overview
text: AquaSensR implements four QC checks reflect widely used sensor data quality standards. underlying concepts code borrow heavily ContDataQC package. threshold values set data quality objectives file can customised per parameter. Manual update thresholds likely necessary avoide false positives negatives. Importantly, flags require manual verification used automatically exclude data without review. DQO columns: GrMin, GrMax (thresholds differ row: Flag = "Fail" vs Flag = "Suspect") Flag column: gross_flag gross range check tests whether observation falls within absolute physical sensor limits. broadest four checks intended catch values simply impossible outside expected operating range instrument. observation compared thresholds two data quality objectives rows parameter: Values GrMin GrMax "Fail" row return "fail" Values GrMin GrMax "Suspect" row within fail bounds return "suspect" fail thresholds define hard physical limits (e.g., water temperature −5 °C freshwater deployment). suspect bounds set somewhat conservatively flag readings unusual impossible. threshold can set NA data quality objectives file skip particular flag. Quickly view many flags type generated gross range check: DQO columns: Spike (threshold differs row: Flag = "Fail" vs Flag = "Suspect") Flag column: spike_flag spike check detects sudden, anomalous jumps (either ) consecutive observations. computes absolute difference reading one immediately , compares difference thresholds two data quality objectives rows parameter: |diff| ≥ Spike "Suspect" row returns "suspect" |diff| ≥ Spike "Fail" row returns "fail" first observation series predecessor always left "pass". spike check flags observation large step, single anomalous reading embedded otherwise stable data generate two flagged observations — one step () outlier, one step back baseline. spike thresholds absolute. example, 5 °C step flagged regardless whether surrounding series calm noisy. rate--change check () evaluates potentially spurious changes relative variability matters. Quickly view many flags type generated spike check: DQO columns: RoCN, RoCHours (Flag = "Suspect" row ) Flag column: roc_flag rate--change (RoC) check adaptive counterpart spike check. title: Flag continuous monitoring data with QC criteria — utilASRflag
text: Applies four independent QC checks selected parameter contdat, matching thresholds dqodat Parameter.  check produces flag ("pass", "suspect", "fail") user can see exactly criteria fired.  Thresholds read two rows dqodat match parameter — one Flag == "Fail" one Flag == "Suspect". Gross range (gross_flag) — Observations GrMin GrMax "Fail" row flagged "fail". Observations GrMin GrMax "Suspect" row (within fail bounds) flagged "suspect". Spike (spike_flag) — absolute difference consecutive observations compared Spike "Fail" row (fail) Spike "Suspect" row (suspect).  second observation jump flagged. Rate change (roc_flag) — observation standard deviation raw values within trailing RoCHours-hour window multiplied RoCN produce threshold.  observation flagged "suspect" absolute lag-1 difference exceeds threshold.  Requires least 2 values window; otherwise "pass".  Note check produces "suspect" flags, "fail" flags.  RoCN RoCHours read "Suspect" row . Flatline (flat_flag) — Counts consecutive observations absolute step previous observation within FlatDelta units.  Observations whose run length reaches FlatN flagged, using "Suspect" row thresholds suspect "Fail" row thresholds fail. Data sorted DateTime processing. Underlying concepts code function borrow heavily ContDataQC package.  credit approach go ContDataQC authors.

title: Flag continuous monitoring data with QC criteria — utilASRflag
code: contpth <- system.file('extdata/ExampleCont1.xlsx', package = 'AquaSensR') dqopth <- system.file('extdata/ExampleDQO.xlsx', package = 'AquaSensR')  contdat <- readASRcont(contpth, tz = 'Etc/GMT+5', runchk = FALSE) dqodat <- readASRdqo(dqopth, runchk = FALSE)  utilASRflag(contdat, dqodat, param = 'Water Temp_C') #> # A tibble: 927 × 6 #>    DateTime            `Water Temp_C` gross_flag spike_flag roc_flag flat_flag #>    <dttm>                       <dbl> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>    <chr>     #>  1 2024-08-14 13:56:33           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  2 2024-08-14 13:56:43           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  3 2024-08-14 13:56:53           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  4 2024-08-14 13:57:03           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  5 2024-08-14 13:57:13           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  6 2024-08-14 13:57:23           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  7 2024-08-14 13:57:33           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  8 2024-08-14 13:57:43           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #>  9 2024-08-14 13:57:53           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #> 10 2024-08-14 13:58:03           24.2 pass       pass       pass     pass      #> # ℹ 917 more rows

title: Apply flatline QC flag — utilASRflagflatline
code: flag <- rep("pass", 8) vals <- c(10, 10, 10.005, 10.002, 10.001, 10.003, 12, 12) dqo <- data.frame(   Flag = c("Fail", "Suspect"),   FlatN = c(5, 3), FlatDelta = c(0.01, 0.01) ) utilASRflagflatline(flag, vals, dqo) #> [1] "pass"    "pass"    "suspect" "suspect" "fail"    "fail"    "pass"    #> [8] "pass"

title: Apply rate-of-change QC flag — utilASRflagroc
text: observation standard deviation raw values within trailing RoCHours-hour window ending (including) observation multiplied RoCN produce threshold. observation flagged "suspect" absolute lag-1 difference exceeds threshold.  least 2 values must fall within window compute standard deviation; otherwise observation skipped.  check produces "suspect" flags; produce "fail" flags.

title: Apply rate-of-change QC flag — utilASRflagroc
code: flag <- rep("pass", 6) vals <- c(10, 10.2, 10.1, 10.3, 15.0, 10.2) datetimes <- as.POSIXct("2024-01-01") + seq(0, 5) * 900  # 15-min intervals dqo <- data.frame(Flag = c("Fail", "Suspect"), RoCN = c(NA, 3), RoCHours = c(NA, 2)) utilASRflagroc(flag, vals, datetimes, dqo) #> [1] "pass" "pass" "pass" "pass" "pass" "pass"

title: Import continuous monitoring data from an Excel file — utilASRimportcont
text: Reads Excel workbook returns data frame Date, Time, DateTime columns preserved character strings, Excel numeric representations converted human-readable text: Date: integer-like strings (Excel date serial numbers, e.g. "45518") converted yyyy-mm-dd using Excel's origin 1899-12-30. Time: decimal fraction strings 0 1 (Excel time fractions, e.g. "0.58105") converted HH:MM:SS. DateTime: numeric strings integer part (Excel datetime serials, e.g. "45518.58105") converted yyyy-mm-dd HH:MM:SS. Text values columns (e.g. "2024-08-14", "4:30:33 PM") left unchanged. function called internally readASRcont can also used prepare data manual use checkASRcont formASRcont.
